subject: [PLATFORM] Add new fake deployment to fix offline certificates generation
commit_message:
[PLATFORM] Add new fake deployment to fix offline certificates generation

Add new fake deployment to CertService, controlled by new global flag global.offlineDeployment, which is disabled as default. Change Makefile to use java image from ONAP Nexus for certificate generation.

Signed-off-by: Adam Wudzinski <adam.wudzinski@nokia.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-2588
Change-Id: I2f9fe4b626604c5bfd8512449d893015bdc6ca98

WHy cannot you just override the entrypoint?;)

Patch Set 5:

make[7]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform/components/oom-cert-service'

11:59:53 docker exec generate-certs keytool -genkeypair -v -alias root -keyalg RSA -keysize 4096 -validity 3650 -keystore root-keystore.jks \
11:59:53     -dname "CN=root.com, OU=Root Org, O=Root Company, L=Wroclaw, ST=Dolny Slask, C=PL" -keypass secret \
11:59:53     -storepass secret -ext BasicConstraints:critical="ca:true"

11:59:55 	for: CN=root.com, OU=Root Org, O=Root Company, L=Wroclaw, ST=Dolny Slask, C=PL

> 11:59:55 	at java.base/java.io.FileOutputStream.open(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 	at java.base/java.io.FileOutputStream.<init>(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 	at java.base/java.io.FileOutputStream.<init>(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 	at java.base/sun.security.tools.keytool.Main.doCommands(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 	at java.base/sun.security.tools.keytool.Main.run(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 	at java.base/sun.security.tools.keytool.Main.main(Unknown Source)
> 11:59:55 Makefile:52: recipe for target 'root_generate_keys' failed
> 11:59:55 make[6]: *** [root_generate_keys] Error 1
> 11:59:55 make[6]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform/components/oom-cert-service'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:35: recipe for target 'make-oom-cert-service' failed
> 11:59:55 make[5]: *** [make-oom-cert-service] Error 2
> 11:59:55 make[5]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform/components'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:31: recipe for target 'oom-cert-service' failed
> 11:59:55 make[4]: *** [oom-cert-service] Error 2
> 11:59:55 make[4]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform/components'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:34: recipe for target 'make-components' failed
> 11:59:55 make[3]: *** [make-components] Error 2
> 11:59:55 make[3]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:30: recipe for target 'components' failed
> 11:59:55 make[2]: *** [components] Error 2
> 11:59:55 make[2]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes/platform'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:60: recipe for target 'make-platform' failed
> 11:59:55 make[1]: *** [make-platform] Error 2
> 11:59:55 make[1]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm/kubernetes'
> 11:59:55 Makefile:44: recipe for target 'platform' failed
> 11:59:55 make: *** [platform] Error 2
> 
> any idea what is causing it?

Maybe it has something to do with permissions. When docker runs, a directory is mounted to the container as volume and all certificates are created in that volume. This exception occurs after the first file is created and FileNotFoundException is thrown, so the file is not created, maybe due to lack of permissions.

Patch Set 16:

OK we fixed it. The problem was bad permission of mounted(binded) directory on container. It had user and group of "1001", but default user on docker(which executed all the commands, like create file or generate certificates) was "onap". Now we set default user to root. And it's working.
